creator: guido@gvr.org
text: The command "whois 65.155.102.0" returns only the first part of the information provided by the ARIN whois server. When you tcpdump, whois connects to port 43 of whois.arin.net and issues the command "+ 65.155.102.0'. The whois server returns  an answer consisting of two parts between "# start" and "# end". But only the first part is printed out by whois(1)
